g.text: Key Insight: 'Slow Start' is a historical misnomer. Compared to sending at full receiver window immediately, it's 'slow'. But exponential growth is actually very fast for safely discovering available bandwidth.

div.p: MSS = Maximum Segment Size (typically 1460 bytes) RTT = Round-Trip Time ssthresh = Slow Start Threshold (dynamically adjusted) cwnd = Congestion Window (in MSS units)

g.text: Per-ACK Formula | cwnd += MSS | cwnd += MSS²/cwnd
Growth Rate | ~2× per RTT | ~+1 MSS per RTT
Each ACK → cwnd increases by 1 MSS

ul.li: Starting at full receiver window Reality: Exponential ramp:

g.text: cwnd += MSS × MSS / cwnd per ACK

div.p: Caution Zone: Network capacity unknown Behavior: Gentle probing:

ul.li: Prevents oscillation at capacity edge Safety: Small increases = small backs-off

g.text: if (cc->recovery_mode) return; // Don't grow in recovery
